nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Walwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Wal(d)wine, which is of ancient Germanic origin, from elements meaning ‘power’ + ‘friend’.

from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Walweyn, Walwain. The central French form of this name is Gawain (see Gavin ). This name is found in the medieval Arthurian romances and is traditionally explained as an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps meaning ‘hawk of the plain’.

variants.name: Gavin
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Gábháin or Ó Gáibhín, both of which are derived from diminutives of gábhadh ‘want’ or ‘danger’ (the second being the more likely meaning here).

Scottish and northern English: from the personal name Gavin, Scottish form of English Gawain and French Gauvain . The name was introduced into English from French versions of the Arthurian romances, where this name was borne by one of the knights of the Round Table, brother of Galahad and Mordred and a nephew of Arthur. It has been taken to be an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps ‘hawk of the plain’.
Scottish: shortened form of McGavin , which is believed to be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gobhann ‘son of the smith’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John de Walwyn was born in 1205, in Much Marcle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas de Walwayne, was 37 and his mother, Leonard Hackluyt, was 27. He had at least 1 daughter with Goffrida. He died in 1260, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Walwyn est né en 1205, à Much Marcle, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de Walwayne, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Leonard Hackluyt, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Goffrida. Il est décédé en 1260, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Walwyn(이)가 1205년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 무치 마클에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Walwayne(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Leonard Hackluyt(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Goffrida(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1260년 영국 잉글랜드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Walwyn родился в 1205 г. в местности муч маркл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas de Walwayne было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Leonard Hackluyt, 27.
